name: VIM Records
description_en:
VIM Records is a Greek electronic label closely associated with breakbeat and adjacent bass-driven club styles. Based in Thessaloniki, it emerged as part of the digital-era network of independent imprints that helped keep breaks culture active well beyond its first commercial peak.

The label presents itself as V.I.M. Records, often expanded as "Very Important Music," and its launch is consistently placed in 2007. From the outset, its identity was broader than a single-genre breaks outlet, but breakbeat remained one of its clearest editorial anchors.

Its catalogue sits in the overlap between progressive breaks, nu skool breaks, electro-house, tech-house, techno and electronica. That range matters: VIM was part of a generation of labels for which breaks was not an isolated niche, but one branch of a wider post-rave, DJ-oriented electronic continuum.

In practical terms, the label became a regular platform for producers working in melodic, rolling and club-focused breakbeat. Its output often favored polished digital singles and EPs, with a sound that could move from atmospheric and progressive to tougher, more peak-time material without leaving the broader breaks framework.

VIM also used compilation formats and recurring series to map its roster and scene connections. Releases under banners such as the V.I.M. Breaks Special Edition line and other various-artists packages helped frame the label not just as a publisher of isolated tracks, but as a hub for a distributed international breaks community.

Although rooted in Greece, the imprint was not limited to a purely local function. It became a channel for both Greek and international producers, reflecting how the late-2000s and 2010s breaks ecosystem operated through online circulation, specialist download stores, DJ support and cross-border label networks rather than through one dominant national center.

Artists associated with the label include Colombo, Rick Tedesco, Alt-A, DJ Ignacio, 1320 and Ellissentials, among others in the progressive and tech-leaning breaks orbit. Their presence points to the kind of catalogue VIM cultivated: functional for DJs, but usually with enough melodic or textural detail to appeal beyond pure utility.

That positioning gave the label a recognizable place within the wider breakbeat landscape. It was not defined by big beat revivalism or old-school hardcore references so much as by the post-2000 strand of sleek, digital, club-engineered breaks that connected with progressive house, electro and bass scenes.

The label's Bandcamp and SoundCloud presence also reflects an important part of its era. Like many independent breaks imprints, VIM operated in a space where online platforms were central to discovery, previews, direct sales and maintaining continuity for a style that often lived outside mainstream dance-media attention.

Within the memory of the scene, VIM Records stands as a durable specialist imprint from Greece that helped sustain breakbeat's international middle ground: not a mass-market institution, but a consistent editorial platform for producers and DJs working across progressive breaks and neighboring electronic forms.
description_es:
VIM Records es un sello griego de musica electronica estrechamente asociado al breakbeat y a otros estilos de club guiados por el bajo y el ritmo. Con base en Tesalonica, surgio como parte de la red de imprints independientes de la era digital que ayudo a mantener viva la cultura breaks mucho despues de su primer pico comercial.

El sello se presenta como V.I.M. Records, a menudo desarrollado como "Very Important Music", y su lanzamiento se situa de forma consistente en 2007. Desde el principio, su identidad fue mas amplia que la de un sello de breaks en sentido estricto, aunque el breakbeat siguio siendo uno de sus ejes editoriales mas claros.

Su catalogo se mueve en la interseccion entre progressive breaks, nu skool breaks, electro-house, tech-house, techno y electronica. Ese rango es importante: VIM formo parte de una generacion de sellos para la que los breaks no eran un nicho aislado, sino una rama de un continuo electronico mas amplio, heredero del post-rave y orientado a la cultura DJ.

En la practica, el sello se convirtio en una plataforma habitual para productores de breakbeat melodico, rodante y enfocado a la pista. Su produccion solio favorecer singles y EPs digitales bien acabados, con un sonido capaz de ir de lo atmosferico y progresivo a materiales mas duros y de hora punta sin salir del marco general de los breaks.

VIM tambien utilizo compilaciones y series recurrentes para trazar su roster y sus conexiones de escena. Publicaciones bajo lineas como V.I.M. Breaks Special Edition y otros recopilatorios de varios artistas ayudaron a definir al sello no solo como editor de temas sueltos, sino como nodo de una comunidad internacional de breaks distribuida.

Aunque estaba arraigado en Grecia, el imprint no cumplio una funcion puramente local. Se convirtio en un canal para productores griegos e internacionales, reflejando como funcionaba el ecosistema breaks de finales de los 2000 y los 2010: circulacion online, tiendas de descarga especializadas, apoyo de DJs y redes transnacionales de sellos, mas que un unico centro nacional dominante.

Entre los artistas asociados al sello figuran Colombo, Rick Tedesco, Alt-A, DJ Ignacio, 1320 y Ellissentials, entre otros nombres de la orbita de los breaks progresivos y de sesgo tech. Su presencia apunta al tipo de catalogo que cultivo VIM: util para DJs, pero normalmente con suficiente detalle melodico o textural como para interesar mas alla de la pura funcionalidad.

Esa posicion le dio un lugar reconocible dentro del paisaje breakbeat mas amplio. No se definio tanto por el revival big beat ni por referencias old school hardcore, sino por una vertiente posterior a 2000 de breaks pulidos, digitales y pensados para el club, en dialogo con escenas de progressive house, electro y bass.

La presencia del sello en Bandcamp y SoundCloud tambien refleja una parte importante de su epoca. Como muchos imprints independientes de breaks, VIM opero en un espacio donde las plataformas online eran centrales para el descubrimiento, las previews, la venta directa y la continuidad de un estilo que a menudo vivia fuera del foco de los grandes medios de musica de baile.

En la memoria de la escena, VIM Records queda como un sello especializado y persistente surgido en Grecia que ayudo a sostener la franja internacional intermedia del breakbeat: no una institucion de masas, sino una plataforma editorial constante para productores y DJs que trabajaban entre los progressive breaks y otras formas electronicas vecinas.
